headline: Oregon NASAO survey confirms 467 drone airframes grounded across 25 states with national exposure between $50M–$2B as regulatory compliance crisis accelerates into Q2 2026.

lead_story.title: NASAO Airframe Grounding Crisis Reaches 467 Units Across 25 States
lead_story.body: Oregon Department of Transportation's NASAO Revision 2 white paper (February 2026) documents 467 drone airframes currently grounded or operationally restricted across 23 states based on a survey of 25 state DOTs. National financial exposure ranges from $50 million to $2 billion depending on replacement costs and procurement timelines. This represents the largest documented regulatory compliance failure in the Blue UAS era, with state and local agencies facing immediate operational gaps in public safety, infrastructure inspection, and emergency response missions. The grounding is directly tied to NDAA compliance failures and FCC Covered List enforcement.
lead_story.action: Audit all airframes in your inventory against FCC Covered List entities (DJI, Autel, Anzu, SkyRover, Cogito/SPECTA) and initiate replacement procurement for any affected units before Q3 2026 budget cycles close.

supply_chain.signal: 96% concentration risk — 463 of 484 tracked propeller models are Chinese-manufactured, dominated by HQProp, Gemfan, and DAL (all Shenzhen/Guangdong). Propellers are not currently classified as NDAA-critical components, creating a regulatory blind spot.
supply_chain.window: 6-9 months before potential regulatory reclassification or tariff escalation impacts procurement
supply_chain.action: Stockpile 12-18 months of propeller inventory for critical missions and identify domestic or allied alternatives (consider carbon fiber manufacturers in US/EU for custom runs).

supply_chain.signal: 97% concentration risk — 187 of 193 tracked FPV cameras are Chinese-manufactured. FPV cameras ARE classified as NDAA-critical components (imaging hardware with data storage/transmission capability), making current procurement non-compliant.
supply_chain.window: Immediate compliance risk — existing contracts may be unenforceable
supply_chain.action: Halt all Chinese FPV camera procurement immediately. Identify NDAA-compliant alternatives from US/allied manufacturers (FLIR, L3Harris thermal modules) even at 3-5x cost premium.

supply_chain.signal: 70% concentration risk — 212 of 303 tracked motors are Chinese-manufactured. T-Motor/Tiger Motors (62 models, Nan'an City, Fujian) dominates with iFlight and BrotherHobby as secondary suppliers.
supply_chain.window: 12-18 months before supply disruption becomes procurement-critical
supply_chain.action: Pre-qualify motors from EU manufacturers (Austrian, German) for next-generation platforms and dual-source all motor specifications in RFPs issued after Q3 2026.

supply_chain.signal: Confirmed diversion to adversary weapons systems — GUR and Ukrainian military intelligence documented Raspberry Pi 4B as core controller in Geran-2/Shahed-136 'Borscht Tracker V3' with 40,000+ units diverted through Kazakhstan and Turkey.
supply_chain.action: Plan for Raspberry Pi export license requirements on all future shipments. Evaluate alternatives (NVIDIA Jetson Nano, Intel NUC) for non-sensitive applications to preserve Pi allocation for defense-critical projects.

gray_zone.status: Added to FCC Covered List with gray zone risk score of 0.850 (CRITICAL) — highest risk entity in database.
gray_zone.development: Teardown analysis confirms SkyRover app connects directly to DJI cloud infrastructure with identical encryption keys. Jon Sawyer identified firmware signed with DJI cryptographic keys and OTA update pipeline routing through adversary infrastructure. Shell company structure traced to SZ Knowact Robot Technology Co Ltd in Shenzhen. Existing S1 ($359) and X1 ($599) inventory still available at retail despite FCC prohibition on new model authorizations.
gray_zone.buyer_exposure: Any agency operating SkyRover S1 or X1 faces data exfiltration risk and firmware compromise via DJI-controlled update pipeline. Estimated 200-400 units deployed across law enforcement and emergency management.
gray_zone.action: Ground all SkyRover units immediately. Treat as adversary-controlled hardware. Forensic imaging recommended before disposal to document potential data compromise for breach notification requirements.

gray_zone.status: Texas DTPA lawsuit filed February 18, 2026 in Collin County with 7 counts; no Anzu response filed after 45+ days.
gray_zone.development: Teardown confirms PCB layouts align exactly with DJI Mavic 3 Enterprise with matching board part numbers. Controller RRC01 is relabeled DJI hardware. Firmware signed and encrypted with DJI cryptographic keys with DJI retaining root key control. Product discontinuation confirmed due to DJI P1 OCU chipset embargo enforcement. Texas seeking injunctive relief, civil penalties up to $10K/violation, and corrective advertising orders.
gray_zone.buyer_exposure: Estimated 2,000-3,000 Anzu Raptor units deployed across state/local government and critical infrastructure operators who believed they were purchasing NDAA-compliant alternatives. Financial exposure ranges from $6M-$9M in stranded assets.
gray_zone.action: Cease all Anzu Raptor operations pending legal resolution. Document purchase justification and compliance review process for potential cost recovery. Contact Texas AG office to register as affected party if seeking restitution.

watch_list.why: 10 of 43 tracked thermal cameras (23% concentration) use FLIR Boson 640, with AeroVironment Switchblade production ramp driving demand in H2 2026.
watch_list.trigger: Lead times extending beyond 26 weeks or Teledyne announcing allocation system would signal critical shortage.

watch_list.why: Autel seeking FCC reconsideration after December 2025 Covered List addition and January 2026 authorization revocations, with gray zone risk score of 0.525 (HIGH).
watch_list.trigger: FCC denial of reconsideration would finalize Autel ban and strand remaining Dragonfish/EVO inventory at state agencies (Florida Sheriff documented user).

watch_list.why: 87% probability prediction of stranded asset crisis when FCC Covered List entities lose OTA update capability in Q4 2026-Q1 2027.
watch_list.trigger: FCC issuing 90-day compliance notice to carriers/manufacturers would accelerate replacement procurement timeline by 6 months.

predictions.event: Anzu Robotics product discontinuation due to DJI P1 OCU chipset embargo enforcement
predictions.hedge: Cancel all pending Anzu orders and redirect budget to Skydio X10 or Teal Golden Eagle platforms with confirmed domestic supply chains. Negotiate early termination clauses on any Anzu service contracts.

predictions.event: FCC Covered List firmware update prohibition creates stranded asset crisis
predictions.hedge: Establish replacement budget reserve of 40% of current FCC Covered List asset value ($20M-$800M depending on agency size) and pre-position replacement platform evaluations to compress Q4 2026 procurement cycle.

predictions.event: FLIR Boson 640 supply tightens due to AeroVironment Switchblade production ramp
predictions.hedge: Lock multi-year Boson 640 supply agreements with Teledyne before Q3 2026 or design next-generation platforms around alternative thermal cores (FLIR Hadley, Seek Thermal) to preserve flexibility.

signal_summary.top_concern: 467 airframes grounded across 25 states with $50M-$2B exposure
